chosen_evolution_point.selection_rationale: Previous iteration used match_phase_exact.cpp (Area Optimizer) and yielded negligible area gain with no delay improvement. With Balanced objective and LOW diversity pressure, shifting to match_drop_phase.cpp targets a low-risk, localized decision point that can unlock delay improvements by smarter per-node phase consolidation. It also aligns with the recommended_explore list and has the lowest recent usage. Compared to match_phase.cpp (broader impact on initial costs) and match_phase_exact.cpp (already exercised), match_drop_phase lets us adjust a single threshold in the delay round to enable beneficial single-phase choices without widespread algorithmic changes.

evolution_step.evolution_point_id: tech_map_impl::match_drop_phase (inside EVOLVE-BLOCK), in the delay round branch `if constexpr (!DO_AREA)`: replace `const float area_tol = 0.0f;`
evolution_step.objective: Allow a tiny area tolerance when collapsing to a single phase (+inverter) if it reduces worst-case arrival, to improve delay with minimal area impact.
evolution_step.direction_and_strategy: Change the strict no-area-increase rule in the delay round to a small tolerance: set `const float area_tol = 0.1f * lib_inv_area;` so the checks `(node_data.flows[1] + lib_inv_area <= node_data.flows[0] + area_tol)` and its symmetric counterpart can accept a slight area increase when the worst_arrival improves.
evolution_step.expected_impact: 0.2-0.8% delay improvement, tolerate up to 0.0-0.3% area increase
evolution_step.constraints: Modify only within EVOLVE-BLOCK; keep APIs/templates unchanged; do not alter cut generation; preserve epsilon-based compares; maintain logical equivalence (failed_rate must remain 0).
evolution_step.rationale: In the current delay round, phase dropping only occurs if it strictly does not increase area. This can block beneficial single-phase mappings that reduce worst-case arrival by inserting a single inverter but cause a negligible area rise. Introducing a tiny tolerance tied to lib_inv_area allows capturing these wins, while subsequent area-flow and exact-area rounds can recuperate area where possible. The change is localized, bounded, and maintains safe tie-breaking, thus matching a Balanced strategy to seek modest delay gains with minimal area trade-off.
